title: fix(createURL): support multi-index
body:
## Description

This updates the signature of the `createURL` function given to widgets' lifecycle (`init` and `render`). It also updates the undocumented top-level `createURL` to match the same signature.

## New signature

The signature at the widget level (given to the `init` and `render` hooks) stays the same:

```ts
createURL(nextState: SearchParameters): string;
```

However, the internal signature in the `index` widget changes and uses the UI state.

```ts
createURL(nextState: UIState): string;
```

This UI state is accumulated with all the widgets mounted on the instance, which results in the complete UI state of the sub indices.

This change is especially needed for generating exhaustive URLs for both `currentRefinement` and `clearRefinements`.

## Current architecture limitations

The new architecture for v4 proceeds top to bottom. This means that if you call `createURL` in a top-level widget `init` hook, it ignores the `uiState` of all children. This results in an incomplete URL string.

This likely will never happen in user code because we rarely only use `init` without `render`. Still, if we've got complains about incomplete URLs, we can suggest adding a `render` method to fix the issue.

## Notes on top-level `createURL`

The top-level `createURL` method has never been documented and was initially introduced for our Magento integration. This is not used anymore so we might consider removing this method by the time we go stable.

See #372 and #377.

## What's remaining

There are some checks that we need to proceed and to fix in others PRs if the result is not expected.

- Check the `createURL` usage in `currentRefinements`
- Check the `createURL` usage in `clearRefinements`
